triggerEvent: User reports persistent React hooks violation despite multiple fixes

sessionContext.originalError: Rendered more hooks than during the previous render
sessionContext.userFeedback: Error persists after multiple fix attempts

failureAnalysis.discovered: Dev server port 5173 already in use, preventing proper hot module replacement
failureAnalysis.evidence: Error: Port 5173 is already in use
failureAnalysis.implication: Component fixes not reaching browser due to failed dev server restart

investigationFindings.browserCacheCorruption: LIKELY - Old component state cached due to failed HMR
investigationFindings.hotModuleReplacementFailure: CONFIRMED - Port conflict preventing dev server restart
investigationFindings.hiddenConditionalHooks: INVESTIGATED - Component code shows proper hook structure

componentStructure.hookUsage: Properly structured with consistent hook order
componentStructure.conditionalHooks: None found in main render path

realRootCause.secondary: Port conflict preventing hot module replacement

effectiveSolution.clearBrowserCache: Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) or clear dev tools cache
effectiveSolution.restartDevServer: npm run dev on clean port
effectiveSolution.verifyHMR: Ensure hot module replacement is working

environmentValidation.portConflictDetection: Test should verify dev server port availability
environmentValidation.hmrStatusChecking: Test should validate hot module replacement functionality
environmentValidation.browserCacheValidation: Test should detect stale JavaScript bundles

componentTesting.hookCountValidation: Test should count hooks in each render cycle
componentTesting.conditionalHookDetection: Test should scan for conditional hook usage
componentTesting.stateCorruptionDetection: Test should detect component state corruption

preventionStrategy.preCheck: Always verify dev environment before applying component fixes
preventionStrategy.tooling: Add port conflict detection to development workflow
preventionStrategy.testing: Include environment validation in TDD workflow

nextSteps: Add port conflict detection to development setup
Create TDD patterns for environment-related failures
